title: Podman stats with no containers listed should throw an error
body: Right now, we allow `podman stats` to run with no containers listed (Reproducer: `podman stats` with no arguments or container IDs/names). This displays a refreshing, empty screen. We should return an error instead, as doing stats on no containers is pointless.

comments.body: I disagree with this.  When no containers are present, stats should run with an empty screen.  This is the docker behaviour and it is consistent with other monitoring tools.
Assuming @baude 's empty screen means showing just a header line, then +1 for me.
@baude Appears to be correct per docker, though the screen should have the header line. Right now, we do not, but Docker does.

No docker behaviour is wrong, lets just exit out when this happens, with a message saying no containers.
Otherwise it is tough to figure out whether the tool failed or their are no containers.

The header should be there... but if this tool is used for monitoring, it would be nice to have it run without a container so you could capture startup, etc.  Sort of like how you can run vmstat 1 and then apply workload.  Otherwise, decide as you will; if you change behavior, make sure you then test and change all behaviours related to it running and having containers stop.
